title: Confidentiality — 15-year-old requesting contraception
candidateInstructions: You are an FY2 GP trainee. Emily Wood, a 15-year-old girl, has attended the surgery alone. She has told the receptionist she wants to discuss contraception. Her parents are not aware she is here. Please consult with Emily.
examinerNotes: Fraser competence/Gillick competence station. Candidate must: assess competence, not refuse to help, not automatically involve parents, apply Gillick/Fraser criteria. Candidate must assess for safeguarding concerns, coercion, consent, safety. Should prescribe combined oral contraceptive or give emergency contraception if eligible. Must not breach confidentiality unless safeguarding concern.

patient.name: Emily Wood
patient.reasonForAttendance: I want to go on the pill. I don't want my mum to know I'm here.

hpi.character: In a relationship with a 16-year-old boy from school, consensual, wants protection
associatedSymptoms: Not sexually active yet but about to be
Knows about condoms but wants more reliable contraception

patient.emotionalState: Anxious, embarrassed. Will refuse to proceed if doctor threatens to contact parents. Mature and thoughtful for her age — understands the information clearly.
patient.communicationStyle: Shy initially but articulate when made to feel comfortable. Will ask directly if the doctor will tell her parents.
hiddenConcerns: I'm terrified my mum will find out — she's very strict
I know exactly what I want and what the risks are
My boyfriend is 16, we've been together 8 months — this is my decision
doNotVolunteer: Boyfriend's age — only if specifically asked about his age
Whether she is currently sexually active — will say 'not yet' if directly asked
How long they have been together — only if asked

dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Understands the information, consequences, implications — demonstrates competence

dataGathering.criterion: Screens for safeguarding concerns and coercion
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Asks age of partner, whether relationship is consensual, any pressure
Explicitly confirms GP will maintain confidentiality if Gillick competent and no safeguarding concern

clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: COCP, progestogen-only pill, LARC, or emergency contraception if appropriate

clinicalManagement.criterion: Explains circumstances when confidentiality could be broken
clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: Serious harm or safeguarding risk — explains this honestly
Gently encourages Emily to consider telling parents — without threatening

interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Treats 15-year-old with respect, does not lecture or moralize
Ensures Emily understands how to use contraception

references.source: Gillick v West Norfolk and Wisbech Area Health Authority [1985]
GMC: 0-18 years: guidance for all doctors (2007)

learningObjectives: Prescribe contraception to a competent minor
